title: Research Projects
text: This project studies how the design of information structures affects decisions, prices, and capital allocation when information is multidimensional and only some dimensions are payoff-relevant.

title: Research Projects
text: This project studies how the design of information structures affects decisions, prices, and capital allocation when information is multidimensional and only some dimensions are payoff-relevant.

title: Research Projects
text:
Market for Credibility
This project develops a theory of credibility as a scarce resource in information markets.

title: Research Projects
text:
Proofs without Disclosure
This project studies strategic communication with privacy-preserving verifiable proofs.

title: Research Projects
text:
Firm Dynamics after IPO
This project examines how financial intermediary heterogeneity affects post-IPO growth, valuation, debt capacity, and upper-tail firm dynamics in Japan.

title: Research Projects
text:
Incremental Value of Data
This project studies the value of data through conditional mutual information and prediction-based decision problems.

title: Teaching
text:
Topics include capital budgeting, cost of capital, capital structure, payout policy, IPOs, M&A, and corporate governance.



Topics include strategy, organization, incentives, decision-making, and data-driven management.



Topics include optimization, linear algebra, probability, and dynamic systems for economics and finance.

title: Research
text:
Working Papers

Payoff-Relevant Information Architecture
This project studies how information structures should be designed when only some dimensions of uncertainty are payoff-relevant for downstream decisions.


Incremental Value of Data and Conditional Mutual Information
This paper develops a decision-theoretic model of the incremental value of data and shows how conditional mutual information characterizes the value of an additional signal.

title: Yosuke Kimura
text:
I am an Associate Professor at Institute of Science Tokyo.
My research interests include corporate finance, firm dynamics, information economics, innovation, and financial markets.
